original-actions: Rides out to confront the approaching demon boar threatening his village
Shoots arrows at the Tatari Gami, piercing its eye and forehead
Kills the corrupted boar god Nago to protect his people
Presents the iron ball extracted from the boar to the village elder
Cuts his hair as ritual severance from his community
Departs his village eastward with only his elk Yakul as companion
Pays a rice seller with gold for provisions during his journey
Shoots an arrow to stop a soldier from killing a fleeing woman
Discovers his curse grants him supernatural strength in combat
Shows the mysterious lead bullet to the monk Jiko-bou at a market town
Learns from Jiko-bou about the western forest and its deity
Rescues two wounded iron workers from a ravine by the river
Tends to the injured porters' wounds before moving them
Carries one wounded man on his back through the forest
Follows the Kodama tree spirits as guides through the woods
Delivers both wounded men safely to Tatara-ba (Iron Town)
Reveals his cursed arm to Lady Eboshi in Iron Town
Confronts Eboshi after learning she shot the boar that cursed him
Intervenes in San's assassination attempt on Lady Eboshi
Subdues both San and Eboshi to stop their duel
Carries the unconscious San out of Iron Town
Gives his crystal dagger to San as a gesture of trust
Refuses to retaliate when shot in the back by a townsperson
Allows San to take him into the forest despite his wound
Asks San to spare the Kodama spirits during their journey
Stands before the Forest Spirit at the sacred pool
Warns Eboshi against killing the Forest Spirit
Attempts to mediate peace between Iron Town and the forest gods
Convinces San that not all humans deserve her hatred
Pursues Jiko-bou to recover the stolen Forest Spirit head
Evacuates Iron Town residents from the Nightwalker's deadly ooze
Retrieves the Forest Spirit's head alongside San
Returns the head to the dying Nightwalker at sunrise
Pledges to help rebuild Iron Town with its survivors
Promises San he will visit her in the restored forest
generalized-actions: Rides out alone to confront corrupted deity threatening home village
Shoots arrows at monstrous creature, striking vital points
Kills corrupted god to protect his people
Presents foreign object extracted from creature to village elder
Performs ritual severance from community before exile
Departs homeland with only loyal mount as companion
Pays merchant with precious metal for journey provisions
Shoots to stop soldier from killing fleeing civilian
Discovers curse grants supernatural strength in combat
Shows mysterious foreign object to stranger at market town
Learns from traveler about distant forest and its deity
Rescues wounded workers from ravine by river
Tends to injured strangers' wounds before moving them
Carries wounded stranger on back through dangerous territory
Follows forest spirits as guides through woods
Delivers wounded strangers safely to industrial settlement
Reveals cursed mark to settlement leader
Confronts leader after learning she caused his curse
Intervenes in assassination attempt on settlement leader
Subdues both combatants to stop deadly duel
Carries unconscious enemy out of hostile settlement
Gives treasured possession to enemy as gesture of trust
Refuses to retaliate when attacked by frightened civilian
Allows former enemy to take him into wilderness despite wound
Asks companion to spare forest spirits during journey
Stands before deity at sacred place
Warns leader against killing deity
Attempts to mediate peace between industry and nature spirits
Convinces wild ally that not all his kind deserve hatred
Pursues thief to recover stolen divine relic
Evacuates settlement residents from deity's deadly corruption
Retrieves divine relic alongside former enemy
Returns divine relic to dying deity at dawn
Pledges to help rebuild settlement with survivors
Promises wild companion to return to restored wilderness
